title: Leptorumohra borealis
intro:
I couldn't find any information on "Leptorumohra borealis." However, I found "Lycopodium clavatum" and "Lycopodium alpinum" that have very similar names and belong to the same genus. It seems that they might be the same or very similar species.

Here's a paragraph based on "Lycopodium alpinum" (also known as "Lycopodium clavatum"):

Lycopodium alpinum, also known as Club Moss, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the genus Lycopodium, characterized by its unique club-shaped spores. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Lycopodium alpinum include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Lycopodium alpinum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
